title: feat: implement weighted health score aggregation
body:
## Description
Implement a weighted scoring system that aggregates all 14 health dimensions into a single composite score. Each dimension (code quality, testing strategy, documentation, CI/CD pipeline, etc.) receives a configurable weight, and the final score is computed as a normalized weighted average. This enables teams to prioritize dimensions that matter most to their workflow.

## Expected Behavior
The `Codebase.CalculateOverallScore()` method should compute a weighted average across all dimension scores. When all weights are equal, the result should match a simple arithmetic mean. When weights differ, higher-weighted dimensions should have proportionally greater influence on the final score.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CalculateOverallScore handles empty dimension lists gracefully and returns 0.0
- [ ] Weights are normalized so they sum to 1.0 before computation
- [ ] Individual dimension scores are clamped to the [0.0, 1.0] range
- [ ] The aggregated score is deterministic for the same inputs
- [ ] JSON serialization round-trips the score without precision loss

Given a set of 14 dimension scores with equal weights
When CalculateOverallScore is called
Then the result should equal the arithmetic mean of all dimension scores

Given a set of dimension scores with custom weights
When CalculateOverallScore is called
Then the result should equal sum(score_i * weight_i) / sum(weight_i)

## Test Plan
- Unit tests in `SlopEvaluator.Tests/` verify edge cases (all zeros, all ones, single dimension)
- Should assert boundary conditions and floating-point precision
- Verify that test covers the scoring model in `Codebase.cs`

## References
- `SlopEvaluator.Health/Models/Codebase/Codebase.cs`
- `SlopEvaluator.Health/Analysis/SnapshotAnalyzer.cs`

title: feat: add mutation testing with Roslyn AST strategies
body:
## Description
Implement the mutation testing engine that applies 10 distinct Roslyn-based AST transformation strategies to source code. Each strategy introduces a small syntactic change (mutant) and then runs the test suite to verify that tests detect the mutation. Survived mutants indicate weak test coverage. The engine should support configurable strategy selection and parallel execution.

## Expected Behavior
The `MutationEngine` should parse C# source files using Roslyn, apply each enabled strategy to generate mutants, compile the mutated code, run tests against each mutant, and report which mutants were killed versus survived. The mutation score is calculated as killed / total mutants.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 10 mutation strategies are implemented and registered
- [ ] Each strategy produces at least one valid mutant for its target pattern
- [ ] Mutated code compiles successfully before test execution
- [ ] Test runner correctly identifies killed and survived mutants
- [ ] Mutation score is computed as a value between 0.0 and 1.0
- [ ] Strategy execution can be parallelized without race conditions

Given a C# source file with arithmetic operators
When the ArithmeticOperatorStrategy is applied
Then each binary arithmetic operator should be replaced with its inverse

Given a set of mutants generated from source code
When the test suite is executed against each mutant
Then mutants detected by failing tests should be marked as killed

## Test Plan
- Unit tests verify each strategy produces expected AST transformations
- Integration tests should assert end-to-end mutation score calculation
- Should test that compilation of mutated code succeeds

## References
- `SlopEvaluator.Mutations/MutationEngine.cs`
- `SlopEvaluator.Mutations/Strategies/`
- `SlopEvaluator.Shared/Roslyn/`

title: feat: implement CLI scan command with JSON output
body:
## Description
Implement the `scan` CLI command that performs a full health assessment of a target .NET codebase. The command should discover all relevant files, run all 17 health collectors, aggregate scores, and produce both human-readable console output and structured JSON output. The command must handle invalid paths gracefully and provide clear error messages.

## Expected Behavior
Running `slop scan <path>` should analyze the target codebase across all 14 health dimensions, display a formatted report with dimension scores and an overall composite score, and optionally write JSON output when `--format json` is specified. Exit code should be 0 on success.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ScanCommand accepts a valid directory path as argument
- [ ] All 17 collectors execute without throwing unhandled exceptions
- [ ] Console output shows each dimension name, score, and grade
- [ ] JSON output format matches the expected schema
- [ ] Invalid paths produce a clear error message and non-zero exit code
- [ ] Command completes within 60 seconds for typical codebases

Given a valid .NET codebase path
When `slop scan <path>` is executed
Then all health dimensions should be evaluated and scores displayed

Given an invalid or nonexistent path
When `slop scan <badpath>` is executed
Then the command should exit with code 1 and print an error message

## Test Plan
- Unit tests verify ScanCommand argument parsing and validation
- Should test that the command invokes all registered collectors
- Assert JSON output can be deserialized to the expected model

## References
- `SlopEvaluator.Cli/Commands/ScanCommand.cs`
- `SlopEvaluator.Cli/Program.cs`
- `SlopEvaluator.Health/Collectors/ICollector.cs`

title: feat: add CI/CD pipeline health collector
body:
## Description
Implement the CiCdPipelineCollector that evaluates the quality and completeness of CI/CD pipeline configurations. The collector should scan for GitHub Actions workflow YAML files and detect the presence of 8 pipeline stage types (build, test, integration, security, analysis, deploy, smoke, performance). It should also evaluate advanced features like caching, matrix builds, concurrency control, and rollback capability.

## Expected Behavior
The collector should parse `.github/workflows/*.yml` files and produce a `CiCdPipeline` model with detected stages, features, and a normalized score. More complete pipelines with advanced features should score higher. A pipeline with all 8 stages and all advanced features should score close to 1.0.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Collector discovers all YAML workflow files in `.github/workflows/`
- [ ] All 8 pipeline stage types are detected by keyword matching
- [ ] Cache usage detection works for actions/cache references
- [ ] Matrix build detection identifies strategy.matrix configurations
- [ ] Concurrency control detection finds concurrency groups
- [ ] Rollback capability is detected from comments or job names
- [ ] Score is normalized to [0.0, 1.0] range

Given a repository with a comprehensive CI/CD workflow
When the CiCdPipelineCollector runs
Then all 8 stage types should be detected and the score should exceed 0.8

Given a repository with no workflow files
When the CiCdPipelineCollector runs
Then the score should be 0.0 and no stages should be detected

## Test Plan
- Unit tests verify keyword detection for each stage type
- Should test scoring with various pipeline configurations
- Assert that malformed YAML does not crash the collector

## References
- `SlopEvaluator.Health/Collectors/CiCdPipelineCollector.cs`
- `SlopEvaluator.Health/Models/Codebase/CiCdPipeline.cs`
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

title: feat: implement scan-improve-verify orchestrator pipeline
body:
## Description
Implement the Orchestrator that drives the scan-improve-verify loop. The orchestrator coordinates the health scan, identifies low-scoring dimensions, applies targeted mutations or improvements, and re-scans to verify the improvements. This is the core workflow that ties together health scoring and mutation testing into an actionable improvement cycle.

## Expected Behavior
The `ImproveCommand` should trigger the orchestrator pipeline: first scan to establish baseline scores, then identify dimensions below a configurable threshold, apply relevant improvement strategies, and finally re-scan to measure the delta. The output should show before/after scores for each improved dimension.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Orchestrator executes scan-improve-verify in correct order
- [ ] Baseline scores are captured before any mutations
- [ ] Low-scoring dimensions are correctly identified using the threshold
- [ ] Improvement strategies are mapped to specific dimensions
- [ ] Re-scan produces updated scores reflecting changes
- [ ] Delta between baseline and improved scores is calculated and displayed

Given a codebase with at least one dimension scoring below 0.5
When `slop improve <path>` is executed
Then the orchestrator should attempt improvements and report score deltas

Given a codebase where all dimensions score above the threshold
When `slop improve <path>` is executed
Then the orchestrator should report that no improvements are needed

## Test Plan
- Unit tests verify orchestrator sequencing logic
- Should assert that the pipeline halts gracefully on scan failure
- Integration tests verify end-to-end improvement cycle

## References
- `SlopEvaluator.Orchestrator/`
- `SlopEvaluator.Cli/Commands/ImproveCommand.cs`
- `SlopEvaluator.Health/Analysis/SnapshotAnalyzer.cs`

title: feat: add documentation health collector with scoring
body:
## Description
Implement the DocumentationCollector that evaluates the quality and completeness of project documentation. The collector should check for README files, XML doc comments on public APIs, inline code comments, architecture decision records, and API documentation. Each documentation type contributes to the overall documentation health score.

## Expected Behavior
The collector should analyze the codebase for documentation artifacts and produce a `Documentation` model with counts and quality metrics. Projects with comprehensive XML doc comments, a well-structured README, and architecture docs should score near 1.0. Projects with no documentation should score 0.0.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] README file presence and length are evaluated
- [ ] XML doc comment coverage on public types is measured
- [ ] Inline comment density is calculated as comments-to-code ratio
- [ ] Architecture decision records in docs/ directory are detected
- [ ] Score is computed as a weighted combination of all documentation signals
- [ ] Collector handles projects with no documentation gracefully

Given a project with XML doc comments on all public types
When the DocumentationCollector runs
Then the XML documentation score component should be above 0.8

Given a project with no README and no doc comments
When the DocumentationCollector runs
Then the overall documentation score should be below 0.2

## Test Plan
- Unit tests verify detection of each documentation type
- Should assert scoring weights produce expected composite scores
- Test edge cases like empty files and binary-only projects

## References
- `SlopEvaluator.Health/Collectors/DocumentationCollector.cs`
- `SlopEvaluator.Health/Models/Codebase/Documentation.cs`

title: feat: implement quality gate command for CI enforcement
body:
## Description
Implement the `gate` CLI command that enforces minimum quality thresholds in CI/CD pipelines. The command should run a full health scan and compare the overall score against a configurable threshold. If the score is below the threshold, the command exits with a non-zero exit code, failing the CI build. This prevents quality regressions from being merged.

## Expected Behavior
Running `slop gate <path> --threshold 0.7` should scan the codebase, compute the overall score, and exit with code 0 if the score meets or exceeds 0.7, or exit with code 1 if the score is below 0.7. The threshold should default to 0.5 if not specified.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GateCommand accepts path and optional --threshold argument
- [ ] Default threshold is 0.5 when not specified
- [ ] Exit code is 0 when score >= threshold
- [ ] Exit code is 1 when score < threshold
- [ ] Console output clearly indicates pass/fail status and current score
- [ ] Threshold value is validated to be within [0.0, 1.0] range

Given a codebase with overall score 0.75 and threshold 0.7
When `slop gate <path> --threshold 0.7` is executed
Then the command should exit with code 0 and display a pass message

Given a codebase with overall score 0.45 and threshold 0.5
When `slop gate <path> --threshold 0.5` is executed
Then the command should exit with code 1 and display a fail message

## Test Plan
- Unit tests verify threshold comparison logic
- Should assert correct exit codes for pass and fail scenarios
- Test that invalid threshold values are rejected with clear errors

## References
- `SlopEvaluator.Cli/Commands/GateCommand.cs`
- `SlopEvaluator.Cli/Program.cs`
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

title: feat: add testing strategy health collector
body:
## Description
Implement the TestingStrategyCollector that evaluates the breadth and depth of a project's testing approach. The collector should detect unit tests, integration tests, end-to-end tests, and measure test-to-code ratio. It should also check for test naming conventions, assertion density, and coverage configuration files. A well-tested project should score significantly higher than one with minimal tests.

## Expected Behavior
The collector should scan for test projects (by convention `*.Tests` or `*.Test`), count test methods (methods with `[Fact]`, `[Theory]`, `[Test]` attributes), and evaluate test quality signals. The resulting `TestingStrategy` model should contain counts, ratios, and a composite test health score.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test project discovery works for standard naming conventions
- [ ] Test method detection supports xUnit, NUnit, and MSTest attributes
- [ ] Test-to-code ratio is calculated as test methods / production methods
- [ ] Assertion density is measured per test method
- [ ] Coverage configuration files (.runsettings, coverlet.json) are detected
- [ ] Score reflects both test quantity and quality signals

Given a project with a test-to-code ratio above 0.5
When the TestingStrategyCollector runs
Then the testing strategy score should be above 0.6

Given a project with no test projects
When the TestingStrategyCollector runs
Then the testing strategy score should be 0.0

## Test Plan
- Unit tests verify test method detection for each framework
- Should assert that scoring handles edge cases (zero production methods)
- Verify that test quality signals influence the final score

## References
- `SlopEvaluator.Health/Collectors/TestingStrategyCollector.cs`
- `SlopEvaluator.Health/Collectors/MetricsWalker.cs`
- `SlopEvaluator.Tests/`

title: feat: implement requirements health collector with scoring
body:
## Description
Implement the RequirementsCollector that evaluates the quality of project requirements documentation. The collector reads from `requirements.json` or `docs/requirements.json` and scores each requirement on five dimensions: clarity, completeness, testability, atomicity, and traceability. High-quality requirements with clear acceptance criteria and code references should score near 1.0.

## Expected Behavior
The collector should parse the requirements JSON file and evaluate each requirement against scoring rubrics. Titles should follow conventional commit format and be between 10-80 characters. Bodies should contain descriptions over 200 characters, Given/When/Then blocks, checkbox acceptance criteria, and references to source files.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Collector reads from both `requirements.json` and `docs/requirements.json`
- [ ] Clarity scoring checks title length and conventional commit format
- [ ] Completeness scoring verifies description length and structural elements
- [ ] Testability scoring detects keywords: test, expect, should, assert, verify
- [ ] Atomicity scoring ensures body length is under 2000 characters
- [ ] Traceability scoring checks for .cs file references
- [ ] Overall score is the average of all requirement scores

Given a requirements file with well-structured GWT-format requirements
When the RequirementsCollector runs
Then the testability score should be above 0.7

Given a requirements file with titles exceeding 80 characters
When the RequirementsCollector runs
Then the clarity score should be penalized below 0.5

## Test Plan
- Unit tests verify each scoring dimension independently
- Should assert that missing fields produce low but non-crashing scores
- Test with requirements of varying quality levels

## References
- `SlopEvaluator.Health/Collectors/RequirementsCollector.cs`
- `docs/requirements.json`

title: fix: ensure trend analysis handles missing historical snapshots
body:
## Description
Fix the TrendAnalyzer to gracefully handle cases where historical health snapshots are missing or corrupted. Currently, if the snapshot history directory is empty or contains malformed JSON, the analyzer may throw unhandled exceptions. The fix should provide sensible defaults and log warnings instead of crashing.

## Expected Behavior
When `TrendAnalyzer.CompareTrends()` is called with no historical data, it should return a result indicating no trend data is available rather than throwing a `FileNotFoundException` or `JsonException`. When some snapshots are corrupted, the analyzer should skip those entries, log a warning, and compute trends from the valid subset.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TrendAnalyzer returns a default result when no snapshots exist
- [ ] Corrupted JSON snapshots are skipped with a logged warning
- [ ] Partial history (some valid, some invalid) produces correct trend for valid data
- [ ] No unhandled exceptions propagate from the analyzer
- [ ] The fix does not alter behavior when all snapshots are valid

Given an empty snapshot history directory
When CompareTrends is called
Then it should return a result with trend direction set to Unknown

Given a history with 3 valid and 2 corrupted snapshot files
When CompareTrends is called
Then it should compute trends from the 3 valid snapshots only

## Test Plan
- Unit tests verify behavior with empty, partial, and fully corrupted histories
- Should assert that no exceptions escape the analyzer boundary
- Test that valid-only histories remain unaffected by the fix

## References
- `SlopEvaluator.Health/Analysis/TrendAnalyzer.cs`
- `SlopEvaluator.Health/Analysis/SnapshotAnalyzer.cs`
